notes: Singular definite article 'the', same surface form as the third-person-singular subject pronoun 'a' (see srn-pron-a-en) but a distinct grammatical function — the same doubling-up pattern already noted on 'den' (plural pronoun/plural article, srn-pron-den). Added to give a per-word translation tooltip (tokenVocabRefs) to unit-04-srn-pronouns.json's example sentence 'A lampu disi na mi lampu.' and fill-blank template 'A sani ___ na fu mi.', per PR #36's review. Not yet cross-checked against a second independent source — tagged needs-verification rather than web-verified until then.

Copula ('to be'), in this sense distinct from 'na' as a locative preposition ('in/at/to') attested elsewhere in Sranan Tongo. Added to give a per-word translation tooltip (tokenVocabRefs) to unit-04-srn-pronouns.json's example sentence 'A lampu disi na mi lampu.' and fill-blank template 'A sani ___ na fu mi.', per PR #36's review. Not yet cross-checked against a second independent source — tagged needs-verification rather than web-verified until then.

Added to give a per-word translation tooltip (tokenVocabRefs) to unit-04-srn-pronouns.json's fill-blank template 'A sani ___ na fu mi.' ('Dat ding is van mij.'), per PR #36's review. Not yet cross-checked against a second independent source — tagged needs-verification rather than web-verified until then.

Added per PR #36's review to give a per-word translation tooltip (tokenVocabRefs) to unit-04-srn-pronouns.json's example sentence 'Pe yu e libi?' ('Waar woon jij?'). Flagging for the reviewer: elsewhere in this repo's own content (srn-ex-4-1, 'Mi e go, yu e go, unu e go.') 'e' is left unmapped as the progressive/aspect marker, not a locative preposition — the same word may be doing the aspect-marker job here rather than 'in', worth double-checking before this is treated as web-verified.
